hypothesis: An external key-value memory bank built from training data bottleneck features can improve cross-site generalization at inference time by providing a learned additive correction signal to the decoder's deepest skip connection, without requiring any fine-tuning or architectural changes to the base model.
change_summary: Implemented a Test-Time Memory-Augmented Adaptation module consisting of: (1) MemoryBank class with FAISS/numpy nearest-neighbor retrieval storing (bottleneck_feature, per_class_dice_weights) pairs; (2) MemoryAugmentedPredictor wrapper that monkey-patches nnUNetPredictor to extract bottleneck features, query the memory bank, and apply an additive correction bias to the deepest skip connection; (3) build_memory_bank.py script to populate the bank from nnUNet preprocessed training data; (4) predict_with_memory_bank.py script for end-to-end memory-augmented inference.
papers_used: Shifting Adaptation from Weight Space to Memory Space: A Memory-Augmented Agent for Medical Image Segmentation
CLoPA: Continual Low Parameter Adaptation of Interactive Segmentation for Medical Image Annotation
